programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tania and Lizzy help a family who are ready to adopt their first dog. Tia rescues a dog that was hit by a car. But it is touch and go whether she can save him before it is too late

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Two warring Sphynxes keep their owners up at night. Plus, Travis and Dorothy fear that their temperamental cat Polly is poised to attack their toddler son Rex

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: A cat named Penny Lane disapproves of her owners room-mate. Felines Roxy and Max begin to attack each other after they move into a new home together

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: In Van Zandt County, Herchman discovers a woman sleeping in her car who may be under the influence of drugs. A man is found with several outstanding warrants

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: The vets care for a cat in Denver that has been mysteriously losing weight. A dog is treated for injuries after being hit by a car

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: A Texas couple ask Pete Nelson to build them a ranch-style treehouse lofted in hackberry trees - it would be the largest project he has ever attempted

programme.title: Bear Woman
programme.desc: Ann Bryant is called in when a 21-stone mother bear charges at a tree surgeon, and she hopes to save a cub from being put down when he is caught breaking into a house

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: It is Thanksgiving on the homestead and the Kilchers have dinner at the old Family Barn. Its time to thanks to friends and family that have helped out this past year

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy rushes to finish his new barn, and makes a shocking discovery. Meanwhile, Jenny gets stranded with her newborn, and the pair are forced to fight for survival

programme.title: Alaska Monsters
programme.desc: The team heads to southern Alaska to investigate sightings of a highly temperamental bigfoot-like creature, and Todd and Rhett build an electrified tepee to catch the beast

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Raneys arrive in Missouri to help a family who have just survived a tornado. Living in a crumbling cabin, they also have no clean water source

programme.title: Air Jaws
programme.desc: Famed shark photographer Chris Fallows embarks on his eighth Air Jaws adventure, setting out to discover how great white sharks manage to hunt in total darkness

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson saddles up to build a treehouse with a Wild West theme. Set in a lone Douglas Fir on the range, he builds the structure with a county jail loft and copper ceiling

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Animal cruelty investigators Andy and Jeana help a skunk and cat trapped together in a storm drain. Officer Kelley finds a snake dumped on a golf course with a horrific injury

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Andy is pushed to his physical limits as he and Cynthia chase an injured dog through the dry canal system. Plus, the officers respond to a cock-fighting operation

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The story of Britain in the 18th century, which saw an explosion of creativity and a country with enough money, from trade and conquest, to pay for it. Sculptor Thomas J Price visits Harewood House to see the elaborate Robert Adam-designed interiors, Joshua Reynolds portraits and Thomas Chippendale furniture that the slave trade paid for. This was also the great age of mockery, and artist Lubaina Himid reflects on William Hogarths scabrous exposure of upper-class hypocrisies, while Stewart Lee analyses the cutting humour of A Modest Proposal, Jonathan Swifts bitter satire about the treatment of the Irish poor, with passages performed by Jason Isaacs

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Michael Bracewell explores the architecture of Surrey, visiting two masterpieces by Edwin Lutyens and the modernistic heart of Gatwick airport the Beehive

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Conductor Ben Gernon joins the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, with Jennifer Pike in a performance of Bruchs Violin Concerto No 1 and Dvoráks New World Symphony. Dvoráks piece takes inspiration from both Native American and African American melodies. The performance is held in Salford

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman presides as the academic quiz starts its 29th series since the revival of the show in 1994, with the opening match of the first round seeing the University of Bristol take on Durham University

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first-ever edition of the quiz rewarding patience and lateral thinking, with the Lapsed Psychologists taking on the Knitters. The teams must make connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.sub-title: The Cursed Valley of the Pyramids
programme.desc: The Peruvian Lambayeque civilisation, which based its culture on a strong belief that building pyramids was essential to its survival - as was human sacrifice. Archaeologists in northern Peru found mass graves near the lost city of Tucume, revealing a bloody secret. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce travels to Florence, Milan, Paris and Warsaw to tell the story of Leonardo da Vincis life, exploring why a man whose reputation rests on only a few pictures - including the Mona Lisa - is regarded as one of the greatest painters of all time. She also visits New York to see a recently discovered piece by the artist

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.sub-title: Walter Sickert and the Theatre of War
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixons assessment of three artists responses to the First World War continues as he focuses on the work of Walter Sickert, an actor and painter who was regarded as too old to fight. Instead, he created edgy, compelling and subtle pictures of those who had also been left behind and were attempting to get on with their lives, despite the conflict and distress around them

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke embark on more cultural city breaks, beginning by heading to the Portuguese capital to see what it has to offer. From a spectacular monument to the maritime globetrotting of Portugals golden age and the work of a photographer documenting the citys large African population, they discover a complex history of former glories and a darker, slave-trading past

programme.title: Scotlands Finest - The Story of the Highland Games
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates a documentary exploring the history and characters at the heart of the tests of strength and speed that constitute the Highland Games, beginning in the Fife village of Ceres, which is said to host Scotlands oldest tournament. The film examines how the tradition started as shows of strength and stamina between warring clans, but was changed into a pageant of tartan by Queen Victoria, and is carried on by Scottish communities around the world

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce travels to Florence, Milan, Paris and Warsaw to tell the story of Leonardo da Vincis life, exploring why a man whose reputation rests on only a few pictures - including the Mona Lisa - is regarded as one of the greatest painters of all time. She also visits New York to see a recently discovered piece by the artist

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The story of Britain in the 18th century, which saw an explosion of creativity and a country with enough money, from trade and conquest, to pay for it. Sculptor Thomas J Price visits Harewood House to see the elaborate Robert Adam-designed interiors, Joshua Reynolds portraits and Thomas Chippendale furniture that the slave trade paid for. This was also the great age of mockery, and artist Lubaina Himid reflects on William Hogarths scabrous exposure of upper-class hypocrisies, while Stewart Lee analyses the cutting humour of A Modest Proposal, Jonathan Swifts bitter satire about the treatment of the Irish poor, with passages performed by Jason Isaacs

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the second of the academic quizs first-round matches, four students from the Open University pit their wits against a team from Newcastle University in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents a quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. This episode, its a battle between the Corssworders and Birkbeck Alumni

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is thrown into dismay when her beloved father goes to the register office to be married, and things go from bad to worse when her neighbour Emmett comes face to face with her uncouth relatives, Daisy, Onslow and Rose

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy escapes his mothers attentions for the first time in his life, but becomes unaccountably jealous when cousin Brinsley wins her favour. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama charts the dramatic story of the political and religious upheavals in Britain following the death of Charles I. Republican leader Oliver Cromwell envisaged a godly kingdom, while Charles II restored the lustre of the monarchy. Subsequent anti-Catholicism during the brief reign of James II led to a pivotal uprising which would transform the country for ever

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: How Silvio Berlusconis ambitious takeover of AC Milan reshaped the club and elevated his own profile, charting a path from footballing triumphs to the brink of political power

programme.title: Saving Venice
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Venetians are turning to revolutionary engineering, restoration of the natural environment and knowledge from their past in order to save their city from the impact of climate change

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britains Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates Britains most secretive and controversial military research base, Porton Down, in a programme originally shown on its 100th anniversary in 2016. He learns about chemical and biological weapons old and new, reveals the truth about animal and human testing, and discovers how the latest science and technology are helping to defend the UK against terrorist attacks and rogue nations

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes series exploring the lives and teachings of influential ancient philosophers draws to a close with a profile of Confucius, who is credited as the first Chinese thinker to take a systematic philosophical approach to issues of social, political and ethical importance. Born during the chaotic Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history, Confucius believed that harmony could be promoted and achieved through the examples of sage rulers. He was also a innovative teacher, and tried to instill his principles of moral excellence in Chinas elite, but died without seeing his vision for his nation come to pass. However, his philosophies would eventually form the bedrock of Chinese culture, with many of his principles of tradition surviving to this day

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.sub-title: The Cursed Valley of the Pyramids
programme.desc: The Peruvian Lambayeque civilisation, which based its culture on a strong belief that building pyramids was essential to its survival - as was human sacrifice. Archaeologists in northern Peru found mass graves near the lost city of Tucume, revealing a bloody secret. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.sub-title: Your Parcels, Lost, Damaged or Late
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the courier company that customers say is still letting them down despite past promises made on the programme. Plus, a second-hand car owner whose vehicle was subject to a recall but still had to pay for its future breakdowns

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Nick Stapleton help the team to stop a group of cryptocurrency scammers stealing money from a man in Kent as part of an elaborate con

programme.title: Dinosaurs: The Final Day with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Robert DePalma has made an incredible discovery in a prehistoric graveyard - fossilised creatures, astonishingly well preserved, that could help change mans understanding of the last days of the dinosaurs. Evidence from his site records the day when an asteroid bigger than Mount Everest struck the planet causing the dinosaurs extinction. In light of these findings, the veteran naturalist reveals exactly what happened, employing CGI to recreate the late Cretaceous period from which he documents, minute-by-minute, the events of that cataclysmic day some 66 million years ago

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple from Dorset to start a new life in Devon, and learns the importance of local newspapers in keeping rural communities connected and informed

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: An intrepid family of four competes for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: JB Gill and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox presents as a team of expert model-makers bring cherished places from the past back to life in miniature. The first episode features Manchesters now-derelict Crumpsall Library, which is recreated for Leah, a retired social worker who met her late husband there as a teenager

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola is shocked when she receives news about a face from the past, Phil and Sam support a loved one, and Ian desperately tries to gain extra votes

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Heat three gets underway, which means six new amateur cooks have left their home kitchens ready to compete for one of four coveted MasterChef aprons. The Signature Dish round features a meal they would serve to impress friends or family, before the next challenge requires them to make an authentic salade niçoise. Previous MasterChef winners Jane Devonshire, Dr Saliha Mahmood Ahmed and Thomas Frake assess their efforts

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Reeling from a sudden loss, Shannon lashes out at her family. Only Ollie can offer her comfort, and as her day takes a surreal turn, she reconnects with herself and confronts her fears, at least until Dylans return snaps her back into a painful reality

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylans new life brings moments of comfort, but he also finds it challenging in a way he never saw coming. His old coping mechanisms no longer work, and he is forced to ask himself what he truly wants. A conversation with Luke reminds him whats at stake and what actually matters. Meanwhile, Cat seeks clarity from her son, hoping to understand the future she wants for herself and her family

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: On patrol in Belfast city centre Adam pursues a fleeing suspect on foot. A few minutes later, he must do it again, as the man bursts out of the police car, trying to escape. Back at the station, Anna and Perri cant resist the temptation to hide the bosss car, temporarily. Meanwhile, the whole team leap into action when a stolen car fails to stop

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts, with actor and comedian Stephen Mangan and journalist Ava Santina Evans joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenboroughs Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: The making of the acclaimed wildlife series that took three years to film, featuring 40 countries and over 600 species. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenboroughs career as arguably the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of all time

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs (1991)
programme.desc: Trainee FBI agent Clarice Starling is sent to interview an imprisoned psychopath with a taste for eating his victims. The inmate offers to help catch a serial killer who is holding a politicians daughter hostage - but to learn what he knows, Clarice has to engage in his dangerous mind games. Jonathan Demmes Oscar-winning thriller, based on Thomas Harriss novel, starring Jodie Foster, Anthony Hopkins, Scott Glenn and Ted Levine

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Fake Review Sites Peddling Dodgy Goods
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Louise Minchin and Julia Somerville investigate the fraudulent websites convincing shoppers to invest in best buy home appliances, and meet the woman who learned too late that her top-rated dehumidifier was not up to the job. Plus, the couple denied boarding when their passports went missing at the airport and the steps to take after being involved in a road collision

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts heads to Wales, where a mixed commercial and residential unit is in surprisingly good condition. Jacqui Josephs house in Norwich is in similarly decent state, while Dion Dublin has a quirky layout to contend with at a three-bedroom mid-terrace in Staffordshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and experts Raj Bisram and Nick Hall join up with two teams antique hunting in Ardingly, West Sussex, before heading to Newbury for the auction. Eric also sails along the Kennet and Avon Canal to learn about its fascinating 200-year history

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The team help a barber in Doncaster, who is working while on the phone, paying out to a scammer. He tries to convince her he can help recover some lost cryptocurrency - and to make matters worse, the team have their work cut out proving to the victim and the fraudster that they are the legitimate ones

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Sea Monster
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist investigates the discovery of a lifetime - the skull of a pliosaur, one of the most ferocious Jurassic predators ever known. This prehistoric marine reptile ruled the seas 150 million years ago, at the same time dinosaurs reigned on land. It measured up at an incredible 12 metres long and boasted phenomenal strength. David has been an avid fossil collector since he was a boy, but he has never come across a find quite like this. He follows two of the UKs most intrepid fossil hunters on their perilous expedition to excavate the skull from its resting place 12 metres up in a cliff face

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps two former international rugby players find a new home in Dorset, and learns about the health benefits of watercress at a specialist farm. Aside from his property hunt, Alistair learns about the health benefits of watercress at a farm which specialises in growing this delicious and nutritious crop

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Flatmates Rachel, Scott, Tara and Daniel compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola is forced to come clean to George, Ian makes a rash decision after being humiliated by Oscar, and Grant gives Linda food for thought with Johnny after Elaine tells her he has money problems

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London, tackling an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charitys ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers, before Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six more home cooks put themselves in the culinary spotlight in the second heat of the week, ready to show judges Anna Haugh and Grace Dent that they have what it takes. The Signature Dish round sees global influences in abundance, with Polish, Mexican and Brazilian flavours making for an exciting tasting, while Grace and Anna set a daunting Classic Recipe test, which requires the cooks to follow a recipe for an egg yolk and ricotta-filled raviolo, served with a sage and mushroom burnt butter

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Acting on a tip-off, the Peelers storm a city centre flat on a drugs bust. Inside, a suspect is startled, cuffed and arrested. Outside, the PSNI team search the nearby streets for the suspected big-time dealer they think may have fled the scene with more drugs. In Belfast city centre, police attempt to arrest a man who refuses to comply with a search and the situation quickly escalates

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Niall invites Ruben to join him and his flatmates for freshers week at university, but what begins with excitement ends in devastating consequences. Written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: As pressure builds ahead of her next fight in Los Angeles, Molly visits a wedding venue with partner Ellis. Paddy discovers a serious shoulder injury that could scupper his next bout

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy discusses his mental health and the struggles and pressures he faces, while Molly trains in Liverpool for the last time before her fight in Vegas

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Sian discovers an account of a spirit revealing secret information during a seance. This new evidence could prove Helens abilities - or that she was a spy. Either way, it seems the authorities wanted her out of the way because she was seen as a danger

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: Documentary following pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The newly regenerated 12th Doctor arrives in Victorian London to find a dinosaur causing havoc in the Thames. Reunited with old friends Vastra, Jenny and Strax, the Doctor investigates a spate of deadly spontaneous combustions, while Clara struggles with whether or not this darker new incarnation can possibly be the man she knew. Peter Capaldi makes his debut as the Doctor in an episode directed by Ben Wheatley (High-Rise, Sightseers)

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the lives of plants in environments around the world, beginning with the plant battlegrounds of the tropical rainforests. New filming techniques allow viewers to enter the plants world and see it from their perspective, and on their timescale. Featuring fast-growing trees, flowers that mimic dead animals, and life and death battles to absorb much-needed sunlight

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals, exploring how they have adapted to their changing surroundings and have found ways to create homes in the wild. The first episode focuses on animals that thrive in the dark, from leopards that use night vision to hunt their prey to bats using echolocation to navigate the night skies

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this documentary following a chimpanzee troop in Senegal. As the dry season sets in, the alpha of the pack, who has ruled unchallenged for the past three years, faces a series of challenges and finds little support from the other apes

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates as a mother puma battels her rivals and tackles her pray, almost three times her size. She must also also endure the wild mountain weather of Patagonia in order to raise her four cubs, which is her biggest litter to date

programme.title: UEFA Womens Champions League
programme.desc: Lyon v Arsenal. Action from the semi-final second-leg tie at Matmut Stadium de Gerland

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives and careers of Paddy the Baddy Pimblett and Meatball Molly McCann, best friends and mixed martial arts fighters from Liverpool as they make their mark on the Ultimate Fighting Championship world. Paddy heads to Vegas where he is due to face off against American MMA star Tony Ferguson

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy is back in Liverpool to see Molly, who is now preparing for her own appearance in Vegas on the back of two losses and is sure her next fight could be the last of her career if she fails again

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates as a mother puma battels her rivals and tackles her pray, almost three times her size. She must also also endure the wild mountain weather of Patagonia in order to raise her four cubs, which is her biggest litter to date

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: Documentary following pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to the South American country of Suriname, which has more forest than anywhere else on earth. He kayaks upriver in search of some deadly critters

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the ocean depths in a submersible then travels to the Natural History Museum to meet some of the weirdest animals from the deep

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Its the halfway point in the race to Mongolia, and after traversing Italy, Greece, and Turkiye, the teams now face the longest leg of the race so far. As they travel through the worlds largest landlocked country, Kazakhstan, and into Uzbekistan, they must navigate the vast Kazakh steppe with its endless horizons and limited English speakers. To keep moving, the pairs have to adapt to a whole new way of travelling. From the shores of the Caspian Sea in Aktau, the racers must embark on a 2,200km journey to Zaamin National Park in the Turkistan Mountains. John Hannah narrates

programme.title: Better Date than Never
programme.desc: Liv hits the beach for her first ever date, while Charles goes sailing. Dianne meets her first match, and Olivias date makes a bold proposition

programme.title: Better Date than Never
programme.desc: Olivia invites her date to meet the family, and Liv shares a passionate moment on the harbour. Dianne ends her first date on a high

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London, tackling an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charitys ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers, before Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces

programme.title: Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary about four rival families - leopards, hyenas, wild dogs and lions - trying to claim a rich corner of Zambia as their home. Leopard mother Olimbas world is turned upside down by the arrival of Wild Dog Storm and her chaotic pack. But just when the dogs are beginning to settle into the Kingdom and welcome a litter of puppies, an even more powerful force appears on the horizon - lions. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this documentary following a chimpanzee troop in Senegal. As the dry season sets in, the alpha of the pack, who has ruled unchallenged for the past three years, faces a series of challenges and finds little support from the other apes

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Molly wins her fight and comes back to Liverpool to see Paddy, who also receives more concerning news about his shoulder injury which could stop his next fight

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Mixed martial artist Paddy The Daddy Pimblett tries to find out if having a baby is as tough as fight camp by getting wired up to a contraction machine. Later, big news from his medical team changes everything

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the lives of plants in environments around the world, beginning with the plant battlegrounds of the tropical rainforests. New filming techniques allow viewers to enter the plants world and see it from their perspective, and on their timescale. Featuring fast-growing trees, flowers that mimic dead animals, and life and death battles to absorb much-needed sunlight

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals, exploring how they have adapted to their changing surroundings and have found ways to create homes in the wild. The first episode focuses on animals that thrive in the dark, from leopards that use night vision to hunt their prey to bats using echolocation to navigate the night skies

programme.title: Here We Are (2020)
programme.desc: When his autistic son is due to move into a home, a devoted father decides to hit the road and run away with his son, delaying their separation and pushing the two on a journey of self discovery. Drama, with Shai Avivi, Noam Imber and Smadi Wolfman. In Hebrew

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents topical debate from Maidenhead, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: As Niall celebrates his wedding, he follows his estranged friend Ruben into a barn, away from all the guests - and in an explosion of violence, he brutally and shockingly attacks him. The story then goes back in time to reveal how two men, who formed such a close bond years before, came to such a point. Drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A woman calls 999 to report that her 11-year-old daughter returned from staying at her dads house and claimed that a stranger came into her bedroom in the night and sexually assaulted her. A forensic team immediately heads to the crime scene, and the house is examined for potential forensic evidence the suspect may have left behind

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At a Wetherby racecourse antiques fair in West Yorkshire, two teams look for lucrative items at an antiques fair. In red are sisters Sharon and Hayley, with their expert Charles Hanson. On the blue team, brothers Harley and Joe are paired with expert Philip Serrell. Danny Sebastian hosts

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the animals that have made a home in a woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley. A tawny owl hunts for rodents drawn to the garden bird feeders, while a dormouse hibernates beneath the fallen leaves. In spring, a queen buff-tailed bumblebee emerges from a winter underground and prepares to build a new colony and a badger mother brings her young above ground for the first time

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Action from the latest matches in the Premier League, including Manchester United v Liverpool at Old Trafford and Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park. United were looking to do the double over their fierce rivals and further enhance their chances of finishing above them in the table. The Villans were aiming to move a step closer to securing Champions League football as they took on relegation-threatened Spurs. Plus, Bournemouth v Crystal Place and a round-up of Saturdays goals

programme.title: The Titfield Thunderbolt (1953)
programme.desc: Villagers try to save their railway branch from closure by taking it over as a public business - but the owners of the local bus company try to sabotage their plans, forcing them to resort to more underhand methods. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway, George Relph, John Gregson, Godfrey Tearle and Sid James

programme.title: Whisky Galore! (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a Hebridean island endure many months without alcohol as a result of wartime rationing. When a ship runs aground carrying thousands of cases of Scotch, the locals are determined to keep it to themselves, despite the best efforts of a puritanical English home guard officer to confiscate it. Ealing comedy, starring Basil Radford, Joan Greenwood, Gordon Jackson, James Robertson Justice and Catherine Lacey

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents live coverage of the third and penultimate session of the final on day 17 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield. Last year, Mark Williams narrowly avoided becoming the first player since Jimmy White in 1993 to lose this final with a session to spare, but he still trailed Zhao Xintong 17-8 going into the evening. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives, ideal to draw upon when planning the perfect picnic. The menu includes Paul Hollywoods pork pies with quail eggs, a superb sandwich from James Martin and Ainsleys own mustard, bacon and onion tarts

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents as the roving team values decorative objects and rarities belonging to the public in Witney, Oxfordshire, uncovering many bargains and a surprise find. Including a portrait by a famous 20th-century painter bought for its frame, an album of watercolours salvaged from a jumble sale, and the interesting discovery of a bracelet of gold eggs

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin revisits some of the historic venues from the series history. He also finds out about the scandalous Georgians of Lydiard Park

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard and Ellie White test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Travelling by rail across South Korea, Michael Portillo explores one of the worlds most successful modern nations, shaped by division, resilience and rapid change. He begins his journey at the demilitarised zone, the heavily fortified border that has divided the Korean peninsula since the end of the Korean War, then travels south by rail into the countrys capital Seoul, which has become one of the worlds most advanced cities

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents live coverage of the fourth and concluding session of the final on day 17 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first player to reach 18 frames will be crowned champion. Mark Williams won the first four frames of last years corresponding session to reduce the deficit to 17-12, but Zhao Xintong then won the one frame he needed to become the first amateur and first Chinese player to win snookers most prestigious title. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Éadaí SOS
programme.desc: Caoimhe Ní Chathail and Proinsias Ó Coinnhead offer style advice to Katelyn, who enjoys the buzz of buying new clothes and doesnt like to be seen in the same outfit more than once. To help combat these habits Caoimhe, Proinsias and the team confiscate her clothes for further analysis, leaving Katelyn with just 15 items of clothing for a fortnight, and shes also on a shopping ban. In Irish

programme.title: The Great Escaper (2023)
programme.desc: Former Royal Navy officer Bernie Jordan is happily settled with wife Rene at a care home. Unfortunately, he hasnt secured a place on the official British Royal Legion trip to France to the commemorate the 70th anniversary of the D-Day landings. Bernie needs to make the trip to Normandy for personal reasons, and with Renes blessing, he sneaks off to pay his respects. Drama, starring Michael Caine and Glenda Jackson in her final film

programme.title: Waves (2019)
programme.desc: High-school student Tyler Williams is a star athlete on the wrestling team thanks to relentless training and sparring with his domineering father, Ronald. Those dreams of excellence are threatened by Tylers shoulder injury, which he secretly deals with using prescription painkillers. Events spiral out of control when Tylers girlfriend, Alexis, learns she is pregnant and the young couple run a gauntlet of angry protesters outside an abortion clinic. Writer/director Trey Edward Shults award winning drama, starring Kelvin Harrison Jr, Lucas Hedges and Taylor Russell

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Vale of Evesham: Bridging the Hungry Gap
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild micro-climate have long made it one of Britains most productive growing regions, with a long history of producing food even in leaner months. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Its the halfway point in the race to Mongolia, and after traversing Italy, Greece, and Turkiye, the teams now face the longest leg of the race so far. As they travel through the worlds largest landlocked country, Kazakhstan, and into Uzbekistan, they must navigate the vast Kazakh steppe with its endless horizons and limited English speakers. To keep moving, the pairs have to adapt to a whole new way of travelling. From the shores of the Caspian Sea in Aktau, the racers must embark on a 2,200km journey to Zaamin National Park in the Turkistan Mountains. John Hannah narrates

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: Part two of two. Long-term addict Bobby heads to rehab, Billy fights for recognition that he has really changed, while Alan is released and heads back to his old ways on the streets of Glasgow

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Theres lots of planting for the plate at Beechgrove Garden just outside Aberdeen as Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce. Kirsty starts work on her competition plot, while Brian gives an update on the vegetables he sowed at the start of the series and is in his rain garden checking on what survived the wet weather in north-east Scotland this year. Plus, the usual handy hints and a visit to Calum Clunies allotment in Leven

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his Japanese adventure, Michael Portillo explores the history of Hokkaidos indigenous Ainu people. Travelling east to the Iburi region, an area closely associated with Ainu culture, he visits Lake Shikotsu. Over a traditional meal of salmon soup on the lake shore, he learns about the beliefs and values of the Ainu and how they were forced to assimilate to Japanese society following Hokkaidos colonisation

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fly-Tippers Get Caught in the Act
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, farmers take a stand against fly-tippers who have been blighting their land with van loads of dumped waste. In Northern Irelands Mourne Mountains, fast moving wildfires threaten grazing land and livestock, as exhausted firefighters and farmers join forces to bring the flames under control. In north Wales, reporter Paul Colgan meets Rhianon Bragg, a courageous survivor of domestic abuse whose terrifying ordeal on her rural smallholding exposed the hidden dangers faced by victims living in isolation

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The team rakes over a jetty, desperately seeking a treasured lost ring, and social media detective Mui gets creative to trace the owner of a phone found in the depths.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today. programme.desc: The newsreader travels 200 miles east of Havana to the historic town of Trinidad. Clive meets the octogenarian carpenter whose skills have contributed to keeping this 16th-century city standing, and learns how to salsa dance with the locals. He also explores the dark history of sugar

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan, Anna Dimitriadis and Vianet Djenguet embark on a six-month expedition following lions, leopards and cheetahs in Botswanas Okavango Delta. Gordon films one of the largest lion prides in Africa as they hunt to feed 28 hungry mouths, while Anna tracks down the regions most elusive big cat, the cheetah. Local wildlife film-maker Brad Bestelink reunites with his favourite leopardess Xudum, but a huge wildfire threatens the resident big cats, and the team

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts visit Mellerstain House on the Scottish Borders and come across some interesting finds, including a Lowry doodle, a dressing case and a valuable majolica dish. Michael Aspel presents

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the RAF Museum at Hendon in north London, antiques experts Anita Manning and Mark Stacey find some real treasures among the bags and boxes to take to auction. Theres a photo album of the facility in the 30s and a rare picture that brings a surprise in the saleroom. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits an exhibition of First World War flying pioneers

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard and Ellie White test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michaels South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the worlds largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsungs headquarters. Touring a futuristic smart home, Michael explores how innovation now shapes everyday life

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Rivers
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the upper reaches of the River Dee, which rises higher than any river in the UK, from the Cairngorm mountains to Royal Deeside. In the heart of the Cairngorm mountains, he learns about writer and mountain lover Nan Shepherds passion for the living landscape. Later on Royal Deeside, Paul is intrigued by the story of John Brown - Queen Victorias very special friend whose photograph and ring she took to the grave

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the most spectacular footage from the second series, capturing wildlife from around the world on film using the latest camera technology, and revealing how they adapt to overcome the challenges posed by living in jungles, mountains, deserts, islands, grasslands and cities

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig chairs the extended version of the quiz and takes a look at vices and virtues with Eshaan Akbar, Jo Brand, Rhys James and Alan Davies

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: A look at how, in 1980, a booby-trapped bomb accompanied by a $3 million ransom note terrorised a casino in Lake Tahoe, Nevada. As the FBI raced against the clock, each step uncovered dark secrets and unexpected motives. This programme follows the FBIs investigations into the identity of the calculating mind behind this sophisticated device and elaborate extortion plan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Peoples Piazza: A History of Covent Garden
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the 400-year history of the public space in Londons West End, which has been a market, a meeting place and a site of protest, performance and renewal. Telling the story of the piazza from its early years as a playground for the aristocracy, through decline and dereliction to eventual rebirth, the programme weaves archive material with virtual production techniques to bring to life the history of this renowned civic space

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best travels along the Foyle river network, criss-crossing his way across the many rivers and streams that make up its great catchment

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at travel in the deaf community, with tips for deaf travellers, solo adventures around the world and stories of confidence, independence and discovery. Presenter Ahmed Mudawi is joined in the studio by stand-up comedian and keen traveller Leah Francisco. Having travelled widely across Europe and Central America, Leah shares how taking the leap to explore the world helped her regain confidence and embrace new experiences

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Amateur cooks including a dairy manufacturer, a medical writer and a maths student enter the kitchen to battle for one of four MasterChef aprons. They begin by producing their signature dish, before following a recipe for a fish finger open sandwich, served with tartare sauce and pickle slaw - the catch being parts of the recipe are missing. Finally, the pick of the crop cook for three diners who know a thing or two about MasterChef - previous winners Chariya Khattiyot and Steven Wallis, and former finalist Omar Foster. Presented by Grace Dent and Anna Haugh

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined by award-winning photographer Becky Bailey. Together, they discuss relationships, communication and how connection evolves over time

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Sarahs slip of the tongue on Alicia Byrds podcast means that shes now gone viral as the wooden condoms girl, so the oversight team decides it would be good if she does a live interview. Around the same time, rumours break on social media about a high-profile footballer, resulting in the team needing to draw up an official statement that everyone can agree on

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Legends of the Pharaohs
programme.desc: Wife to King Akhenaton, Nefertitis portrait bust - now located in Berlin - is one of the most well-known ancient sculptures in existence. Nefertiti means the beautiful woman has come, and according to 14th-century BC rumours, she lived up to her name

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds
programme.desc: A remotely operated vehicle (ROV) finds the remains of a massive skeleton 825 metres below the surface of the Mediterranean. Plus, a building that appears to shrink as a family gets closer to it and a strange worm-shaped object spotted in the sky

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Spontaneous Human Combustion, the Tell-Tale Hum, and UFO Explosion
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts see the black and white photographs taken in 1951 that depict a woman in St Petersburg, Florida, spontaneously combusting. Plus, a brain-numbing noise plaguing residents in Brooklyn and a woman who claims to have witnessed something even more frightening than a Chinese spy balloon

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Swamp Beast Sighting, Martian Conspiracy and Transparent Mexican Sea Monster
programme.desc: An investigation into a remarkable sighting of the skunk ape, a creature often likened to Bigfoot. A canoer claims to have captured footage of this elusive beast navigating through gator-infested waters, raising questions about what may be lurking in the swamps. Plus, the discovery of a peculiar transparent sea monster off the coast of Mexico, which has sparked the curiosity of marine enthusiasts and experts alike

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts tells the story of Tudor England by studying the history of Norwich, learning the harsh reality of religious intolerance, uncovering the origins of Norwich FCs iconic canary and experiencing humiliating Tudor justice. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the skies to see how the current layout reflects its history and, at the conclusion of the episode, cutting edge CGI is used to recreate how Norwich would have looked during this period of British history

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts tells the story of the Regency period by studying the history of Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town. She attends a period society ball, investigates the rigid social hierarchy of the age and explores the Regency fondness for enemas. The penultimate episode of the series in which Alice Roberts studies one key period in history by delving into the secrets of a historic town that encapsulates the era, providing an accurate impression of what life was really like at key moments in the turbulent past

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is joined by antiques dealers Ian Towning, Cheryl Hakeney, Mark Stevens and Karen Dalmeny in Birmingham, where they appraise collectibles and heirlooms brought along by members of the public, including a mourning ring, a jewellery box and a barometer

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A vintage Versace collection stuns Chum, while a guitar signed by Aerosmiths Joe Perry is brought in. Plus, Corey unboxes a skateboard signed by Carrie Fisher

programme.desc: At storage auctions across Canada, high-stakes buyers convene daily. In this edition, the buyers compete for the attention of Don the auctioneer

programme.sub-title: Imitation Is the Sincerest Form of Insult
programme.desc: At storage auctions across Canada, high-stakes buyers convene daily. In this edition, Roys Paul-disguise causes mayhem at an auction

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Barry believes all the whales are coming to the big auction in Fullerton as he takes Dusty under his wing to test him. Rene makes some dainty discoveries, while Darrells tool locker becomes quite eye-opening

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The women outnumber the men in Rosamond, as Dusty and his girlfriend discover a stockpile of vintage TV memorabilia. Brandi enlists the help of Danielle and Lisa to dig for gold

programme.title: Storage Hunters

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: Despite being one of the most studied civilizations, rich in history, culture, science and art, much of Ancient Egypt remains a great mystery to this day. This series uses the latest science and cutting-edge technology to help experts solve some of Egypts most enduring mysteries that have tormented scientists for years. Here, science reveals new insights into belief and superstition in the land of the pharaohs, from finding the long-lost Land of Punt to Tutankhamun

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: The lives of Brits seeking a fresh start in the Sunshine State. In this first episode, its opening night at the brand-new Best of British pub near Orlando where owners Martin and Beverley have poured their savings and their hearts into their new business. Over in St Pete Beach, Dan is working hard on his plan to become an estate agent in Floridas competitive high-end real estate market

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Vivienne and Simon attempt to transform a peeling, lifeless room into their deluxe Empire Suite, with beds to upcycle, giant mirrors to install and the entire space to re-invent

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Sharon and Shaun Garner meet friends whose search for a modern bolthole comes with one firm rule - absolutely no terracotta floors. But finding the perfect mix of community, location and style proves trickier than expected, as one property splits opinion and another raises the stakes

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at some of their best builds, exploring stories where homeowners were shown ways to make their budgets stretch to cover complex plans. Featuring a couple in Albrighton, Shropshire, divided between enjoying the fruits of their renovated three-bed terrace and moving on to a new project

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Documentary set inside Woodgreen animal charity, following staff who are committed to matching their homeless dogs with prospective owners. Bulldog Commander Rockerfella - aka Rocky - is sporting a new look for his meeting, after a much-needed facelift. Dafne the sausage dog is pining after losing her human soulmate - and is not sold on plucky papillon cross Woodys moves to impress her. Muddy puddle enthusiast Grady the lurcher is always looking for her next thrill, however Woodgreen staff think that adventure seeker Denise could be the perfect match

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The course enters the high-stakes Dating Phase. Guided by experts, the virgins learn to escalate and navigate consent. Tegan is tested by her struggles with self-acceptance, and Ellens extreme anxiety makes her question if dating is for her. Alex has a breakthrough in an intimate session with Ilil, and for two members of the group, sparks begin to fly for real during dating practice

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: The first of four documentaries exploring stories of romance fraud, exposing the love, lies and jaw-dropping tactics of heartless manipulators and the fight to bring them to justice. Megan was swept off her feet by charming Lord Bertie Underwood, who arrived on the Isle of Wight and showered her with gifts and luxury trips away. Before long, the pair were engaged. However, her world started to fall apart when she found his wallet with numerous IDs under different names. Worse was to come when she discovered he had taken out numerous business loans in her name - and even her engagement ring proved to be fake

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 56-year-old woman is rushed to St Georges with an open ankle fracture after falling in her garden, and a man suffering chest pains looks back on his career on the railway, which was cut short by a leg amputation. An 84-year-old man collapses at home following a bout of dizziness, and his children reflect on the challenges of watching his health decline in recent years

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Three-part documentary telling the story of the 2018 poisoning of ex-Russian spy Sergei Skripal and his daughter Yulia in the quiet city of Salisbury, an incident that sent shockwaves across the globe. Just months later, British mother of three Dawn Sturgess died accidentally after coming into contact with Novichok, the nerve agent used in the original attack. Episode one explores the poisoning of Skripal - a reckless assassination attempt that put thousands of innocent lives in danger; and the efforts of those on the frontline to solve the investigation before anyone else fell ill

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: The no-nonsense chefs covert inspection of barbecue joint Wilsons Secret Sauce unveils a jumbled menu with more than 75 items and a pantry filled to the brim with wasted brisket. Meanwhile, the front of house is heavily understaffed, with unreasonable waiting times for customers. Can Gordon help owner Steve and his wife Kelly to strip back the overwhelming menu and massive portions, while also improving customer service?

programme.title: Outrageous Homes
programme.desc: Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen meets a couple whose home and attire is gothic vampire fantasy. From their black lace four-poster to their red-tiled, red-grout bathroom, everything is on-theme, and they have a walk-in wardrobe to die for. We also meet Claire at her teeny-tiny Welsh cottage with maximum impact, and Will and George who love to live like a rich 1980s woman in her 40s. Laurence also visits Mary Rose, a well-known potter whose home is bursting with every colour. In Nottingham, he jumps back in time to a 1900s house with its very own corner shop, and he heads to Scotland where one homeowner has gone to extraordinary lengths to decorate her ceiling

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In Manchester, George meets a family of five with a Victorian coach house thats more 1960s than 1860s. He also helps a young couple in Hove get to grips with a 16th-century gate house with a kitchen-diner that resembles a Tudor banqueting hall

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples in Plymouth battle to be the best dinner-party hosts and win a £1,000 prize. The pairings taking part are Steve and Caroline, who hope to impress with their complex Middle-Eastern menu, newlyweds Jade and Seb, who must try hard to keep their guests happy, and foodies Bev and Nick

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Ste has a big decision to make, and Leela is furious when she finds out what hes been up to. Diane enlists Leelas help, and the fourth member of the Keane family arrives in the village. Cleo struggles with guilt, Rex does whatever he can to keep Ste and a mother selflessly tries to push two residents together

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with actress and comedian Nina Wadia in Dictionary Corner, as contestants pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: In a strange reversal of their usual attitude, Diane and Carla compete to be Cliffs date for the postmans ball. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson, Shelley Long and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam tries to bolster Frasiers ego by arranging a date for him - with a girl who likes men but is not too choosy about them. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kelsey Grammer, alongside George Wendt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm refuses to tarnish the reputation of a promotion rival at work, while Frasier tries to awaken Dianes romantic feelings with a meticulously planned slide show. Bar-room comedy, starring George Wendt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra shocks Ray by revealing she would like more time alone, making him fear their marriage may be heading for the rocks. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra plays amateur psychiatrist to discover why a convalescing Robert is driving everyone crazy with his mean-spirited behaviour. American comedy, starring Brad Garrett and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra suffers from PMT, so Ray decides to help alleviate her symptoms - but only succeeds in making matters worse. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin persuades Frasier to take part in a charity auction, where he is thrilled to be bought by a model - but all she wants him to do is babysit her daughter. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: You Cant Tell a Crook by His Cover
programme.desc: Daphne goes on a date with an ex-convict, but fortunately the Crane brothers are close at hand to protect her honour. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: The Show Where Lilith Comes Back
programme.desc: A convention brings Frasiers ex-wife Lilith to Seattle, but he discovers there is a hidden agenda behind her visit. Comedy, guest starring Bebe Neuwirth

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne offers to cook a romantic dinner to help Niles make amends with Maris after an argument - but ends up stranded with him on a stormy night. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jerseys Spanish Pavillion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eaterys fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole head to Norfolk, where Simon converts a trough into a bioethanol patio heater and Henry uncovers a vintage stationary engine

programme.title: Matt Bakers British Isles
programme.desc: Matt Baker explores the four nations of the UK. In this first episode, he heads to Kent to meet the Englishman teaming up with one of the worlds most prestigious champagne houses to realise a dream of making the first true Anglo-French sparkling wine. In the Highlands, Matt meets two ex-farmers whose lives are entwined with the village of Crathies church

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by actress and comedian Nina Wadia in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: School club manager Michelle and decorator Steve seek a holiday home in or around the beautiful villages of Haute Vienne, in southern France, for all their family to enjoy. Sara Damergi shows them the type of places that their budget of £100,000 can buy

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: The lives of Brits seeking a fresh start in the Sunshine State. The staff of the Best of British pub in Orlando add afternoon tea to their offering, only for the scones to suffer in the 40-degree heat. Estate agent Denise tries to sell her cherished former family home and beauty editor Donna prepares for a big TV break

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul race to waterproof their basement of their once-derelict five-floor home as river levels rise in Brittany. Eleanor and Ben explore different châteaux near the Pyrenees that they hope will lead them to a forever family home. Vivienne and Simon plan a lavish costume party themed around their new Empire Suite

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sophie and Charlotte Rodriguez help Katy and Mo move one step closer to their ideal lifestyle in Alicante. Away from the viewings, tensions rise in the office as Damons company quiz brings out the sisters competitive streak

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Jamies Ultimate BBQ
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver presents tips on improving peoples grilling skills. He starts with a juicy sirloin steak cooked three ways, accompanied by chargrilled salad, with smoky flavours and textures only a barbecue can deliver. He also cooks up a chicken Caesar salad with charred vegetable and bacon, turns Italian-inspired lamb chops into sizzling lamb lollipops, and has a few tips for grilling a whole fish

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The nudity phase begins, giving the group members a chance to confront body insecurities and become more familiar with their anatomy. Katie faces painful memories of bullying, Bertie is keen to have his first kiss and Tegan takes a major step towards intimacy with Risdon, experiencing connection through touch like never before

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part mission, the chef gains insight on Callahans Seafood Bar and Grill, which has been sinking fast since the owner died and left the restaurant to his three daughters. The business used to be the centre of their family bond, but eldest daughter Anikas sub-standard leadership and the kitchen staffs lack of respect have left the restaurant - and the sisters relationship - in turmoil. Gordon begins by compelling the sisters and their staff to take accountability

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the nations top personalities turn their hand to being the countrys most opinionated viewers as they critique the weeks TV. Giving their view on the most talked about television of the week are Davina McCall with her partner Michael Douglas, Katherine Ryan and her husband Bobby, Jane McDonald and friend Sue, Rob Beckett and Tom Allen, Tom Grennan and his dad Martin, Munroe Bergdorf and Leomie Anderson, Stephen Mangan with his sister Anita, Chris Packham CBE with his step-daughter Megan, Big Zuu, Hyder and Tubsey, Paul Sinha and husband Olly, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Clare Balding and Alice Arnold. This time, they share their opinions on The 1% Club, Spy in The Ocean, Fake Profile, Five Star Kitchen: Britains Next Great Chef and The Yorkshire Vet

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Both of the Great North Air Ambulances helicopters are dispatched to help a critically injured father and his two young sons, following a car collision in Cockermouth. The crew from Thames Valley Air Ambulance head to Bracknell, where Martin is having breathing difficulties. Plus, in Stanford-le-Hope, gardener Graham has a nasty open ankle fracture after falling off a ladder

programme.title: Wonders of the World I Cant See
programme.desc: Chris McCausland is joined by comedian and presenter Tom Allen in Rome, where they eat ice cream before it melts, zip around the city on a rickety Vespa, train to be gladiators, learn to sing opera and make pasta from scratch. And will Toms one-of-a-kind commentary do the Colosseum justice for Chris?

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sandi is joined by Eddie Izzard on an Irish adventure as they visit three holiday hideaways in Counties Clare, Kerry and Cork and bond over homemade apple crumble. Their first stop in County Clare is a miniature modern marvel called Teeroneer, a timber-clad, prize-winning property with a sunken kitchen that Sandi just cant resist cooking in. From there the pair head to Ark Ranch Treehouse. Hand-built by one woman, Sandi and Eddie are charmed by the sense of childhood adventure they feel. Finally, they head to Lost Cottage in the mountains and lakes of County Kerry. A classic fusion of old and new, the remote rental gives the pair an excuse for Sandi to take Eddie on her very first fishing trip

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Lelas, a Pomona, California, restaurant that is currently being run into the ground by its inexperienced owner. Gordon tries to turn the failing business around, but faces opposition from the chef and makes enemies when he exposes the suspect behaviour of some members of staff

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Three-part documentary telling the story of the 2018 poisoning of ex-Russian spy Sergei Skripal and his daughter Yulia in the quiet city of Salisbury, an incident that sent shockwaves across the globe. Just months later, British mother of three Dawn Sturgess died accidentally after coming into contact with Novichok, the nerve agent used in the original attack. Episode one explores the poisoning of Skripal - a reckless assassination attempt that put thousands of innocent lives in danger; and the efforts of those on the frontline to solve the investigation before anyone else fell ill

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Three spooky tales for the shows 29th Halloween special, featuring the town overrun by plant bodysnatchers, Lisa snapping, and Homer and Cthulhu clashing in fishy circumstances

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie and Alice Powell present action from the fourth round of the campaign, held at Miami International Autodrome in Florida. Mercedes have dominated the opening round of the season, with George Russell winning the first race and Kimi Antonelli, but it was McLarens Oscar Piastri who prevailed here last year

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordon arrives at Marvel Ranch in Pennsylvania, a family owner diner that has operated for more than four decades but is close to shutting its doors for ever. The owner Cheryl practically grew up in the restaurant but is on the verge of bankruptcy after buying the business from her father. Despite this, she continues to care to care for her dysfunctional staff, who are too busy messing around to give her anything in return. Can Gordon tame the brawling staff and transform the sloppy kitchen crew into proper chefs?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and rebellious new friend Ruth set off for a girls night out - but end up being pursued at high speed through the streets of Springfield by Chief Wiggum and a distraught Homer

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart joins an organisation called the Junior Campers and asks Homer to come on a father-son rafting expedition. Unfortunately, the pair soon find themselves adrift on the open sea - with only Ned Flanders and his children for company. With the voice of Ernest Borgnine

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Sarah Beenys New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn start work on their dream farmhouse kitchen-diner and Pete is shopping for a tractor for his familys new smallholding

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam decides to donate one of his baseball jerseys to a charity auction, but it fails to attract any interest until Diane takes pity on him and secretly bids for it. Ted Danson stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A quiet, unnerving customer begins frequenting the bar, driving everyone crazy as they try to work out what he wants. Guesses range from a paternity suit against Sam, an Inland Revenue audit of Norm, and a postal investigation of Cliff, but the truth turns out to be far more mundane

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane and Sam try to help a dejected Frasier rebuild his self-confidence by giving him an intriguing relationship to study - their own. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Rays efforts to take an interest in one of Allys hobbies backfire, leaving him searching for a particularly rare trading card to make amends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Madylin Sweeten and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra finally prepares a palatable meal, much to the surprise of her long-suffering family, who have grown accustomed to a steady stream of culinary disasters

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra decide it is high time Marie and Frank had a social circle of their own, and encourage them to make new friends - but the plan backfires. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Miracle on Third or Fourth Street
programme.desc: Frederick announces he is unable to visit at Christmas, prompting a dejected Frasier to volunteer to work over the festive period. Unfortunately, he soon finds his callers are even more depressed than he is, and seeks solace in a late-night diner. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin spends a romantic evening with a neighbour - to the surprise of Frasier, who causes family friction by discussing their date on his radio show. Meanwhile, Roz goes out with a Star Trek fan. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie and Alice Powell present action from the fourth round of the campaign, held at Miami International Autodrome in Florida. Mercedes have dominated the opening round of the season, with George Russell winning the first race and Kimi Antonelli, but it was McLarens Oscar Piastri who prevailed here last year

programme.title: The Kid Who Would Be King (2019)
programme.desc: Alex is a 12-year-old lad who is starting a new term at school and trying to adapt to his new surroundings. While hiding from bullies in a construction site, he finds a mysterious sword and discovers its King Arthurs Excalibur. Eventually Alex meets legendary wizard Merlin and faces up to a tense battle with the evil Morgana. Joe Cornishs fantasy action adventure, starring Louis Ashbourne Serkis, Rebecca Ferguson and Tom Taylor.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with actress and comedian Nina Wadia in Dictionary Corner, as contestants pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps carpenter Bob and sports massage therapist Vida, whove been together for 20 years. The west coast of the Peloponnese region in Greece is a very special place for Bob, whos visited the area for 40 years. The couple want to buy a holiday home that one day could become their permanent home. Laura shows them five options for their £300,000 budget
