programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When Murdoch and Crabtree participate in a battlefield re-enactment, one of the participating soldiers is murdered

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A feature-length special to conclude the anniversary celebrations, which stretches from a grey, damp and drizzly London all the way to the sparkling beauty of idyllic Saint Marie. A wealthy shipping magnate is found dead at a Christmas party. Things get stranger when a man in London receives a mysterious Christmas card connected to the victims death. Danny John-Jules makes a return as charismatic Officer Dwayne Myers, with special guests Mathew Baynton, Juliet Stevenson, Stanley Townsend, Jocelyn Jee Esien, Anthony Calf, Tessa Bonham Jones, Elizabeth Tan, Tariq Jordan and Sara Cox joining the regular cast

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A series of murders occurs after Murdoch and Ogdens book is released, prompting a desperate search for the copycat killer

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Darkness Before the Dawn - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A constable is shot in a botched raid on an opium den led by Watts, and Murdoch suspects John is being framed for murder by Inspector McWorthy

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Darkness Before the Dawn - Part Two
programme.desc: Murdoch suspects John is being framed for murder by Inspector McWorthy and Brackenreid goes after the corrupt copper

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: In a special musical episode, Murdoch takes a bullet to the back of the head and slips into a coma where everyone in his world bursts into song

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A lively evening on the Starbright Lounge dance floor erupts into a fight - with deadly results.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of an estate agent, who has been killed ahead of the potential sale of local beauty spot St Agathas Cove. Marlon leads a counterfeit goods case to impress the Commissioner, while love is in the air for Neville following an unexpected encounter. Ralf Little, Don Warrington and Ginny Holder star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: In Saint Barnabas, Naomi attends the wedding of her best friend Monique. However, the jollity of the day comes to an abrupt halt when Rex, the estranged husband of Moniques mother Odette, is fatally stabbed. Knowing the case requires full investigation, Naomi calls the team for help. Meanwhile, Marlon tries to prepare for his sergeant exams but is easily distracted. Ralf Little, Don Warrington and Shantol Jackson star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Vincent Petit, the former resident of a childrens home, meets a grisly end while revisiting the establishment to deliver a speech about how his time there helped shape his life. Darlene and Marlon track prime suspect Marianne, who was spotted at the scene with a knife in her hand. At the bar, Selwyn meets daughter Andrina for the first time. Ralf Little, Don Warrington and Ginny Holder star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Neville receives an anonymous letter telling them a murder is about to be committed. Not long after, he and Naomi are called to the harbour when a body is pulled from the ocean. The victim suffered blunt trauma and it is soon suggested that his disgruntled wife may be a person of interest. Marlon returns after taking a short holiday following his disappointing exam results. Sun-kissed detective drama, starring Ralf Little, Don Warrington and Ginny Holder

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Following Nevilles arrest on suspicion of killing David Cartwright, DI Karen Flitcroft from the Department of Policing Standards arrives to investigate. CCTV footage shows an agitated Neville visiting the victim shortly before he was found dead, which as far as Flitcroft is concerned proves his guilt - so she advises the team members that if they are convinced of their inspectors innocence, they must prove it quickly. Robert Webb guests stars in the crime drama, with Ralf Little

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a renowned calypso singers husband. Neville believes it is poison since the paramedics found blue traces on the mans fingers, but there are several lines of inquiry to follow. Away from the case, Neville struggles with Sophies betrayal and informs the Commissioner that he intends to leave Saint Marie in order to extract himself from various reminders. Ralf Little and Don Warrington star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victims family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby Citys Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede

Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a QI look at some vets with Tom Allen, Melanie Bracewell, Desiree Burch and Alan Davies

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Wealthy family members lose their fortune after being defrauded by their business manager and move to the small town they bought for their son as a joke birthday gift in 1991. First episode of the comedy, starring Eugene Levy

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Johnny searches for permission from the mayor to sell the town, while David and Alexis enjoy a night out with the locals

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Johnny tries to have an potentially offensive sign removed, while David is faced with the terrifying task of searching for a new job

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Alexis does her community service with Mutt, while Moira and Johnny contemplate whether or not they are bad parents and attempt to get to know their grown-up children better

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are sent to Bolivia, where they are challenged to travel through the rainforest and journey to the coast of Chile, driving second-hand off-road vehicles bought locally for a maximum of £3,500 each. Along the way, they encounter drug lords and feel the effects of altitude sickness as they negotiate the regions roads, one of which is a notoriously dangerous 40-mile mountain pass dubbed El Camino de la Muerte - the Death Road

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to America for an interstate road trip, completing various challenges along the way. Starting in Miami, they each buy a car for less than $1,000 and drive to New Orleans, where they set out to give their vehicles away to people who suffered in Hurricane Katrina

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a road trip, trying to follow the path of the Three Wise Men across the Middle East. However, they opt to do so in open-top sports cars that were built for significantly less demanding tasks than driving along the conflict-ridden mountains of southern Turkey, through war-ravaged Iraq and across the searing deserts of Syria. The trio also try to find out what myrrh is and where it can be bought

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon to a big welcome from both their families. However, Smithy seems disconcerted by his pals return and Nessa makes a confession that spoils the happy occasion. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne, James Corden and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an Essex wife - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: After a day spent house-hunting, the newlyweds take up an invitation from Nessa and Smithy to relax with a trip to the bowling alley. However, when tensions between Gavin and Stacey reach a head at the end of the night, their two friends begin to regret making the offer

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Hapless uncle Bryn organises a special treat for Gwens birthday. However, his supposedly covert actions do not go as unnoticed as he would like, and an unexpected guest arrives. Meanwhile, Stacey tries to summon the courage to tell Gavin of her big decision

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds begin leading separate lives back in their home towns. In Essex, Smithy makes efforts to remind Gavin of the excitement of his bachelor years with a traditional boys night out. Meanwhile in Barry, Stacey tries to distract herself with a trip to the bingo

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Nessa goes into labour a month early in Barry, and the newlyweds must forget their differences to track down Smithy in time for the birth of his child. Meanwhile, Stacey returns to Essex to salvage her marriage

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef champions the food, history and culture of the county, foraging for mussels in his favourite rock pools, and touring Bodmin Moors dark past with his son Jack. He also cooks a fool-proof souffle with artisan Cornish goats cheese

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick makes heritage cider with Raynor Winn, author of The Salt Path, and cooks chicken, leek and cider gratin for his friend John Harris, the head gardener at Tresillian House, who regales Rick with his memories of cider-making as a boy. The chef also meets a sea shanty group with a difference

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. Guests Miranda Hart, Stephen Mangan, Barry Cryer and Miles Jupp join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo, actor Tom Davis, actor and musician Shaun Ryder and comedian Henning Wehn, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.desc: Previous winners Maggie Aderin-Pocock, Cariad Lloyd, Gareth Malone and Mike Wozniak test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.desc: Richard Ayoade investigates whether he can make himself a brighter, better man with the aid of gadgetry. programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A woman is caught trying to enter the US with a baby that isnt hers, a man wanted for a serious crime is intercepted, and a driver has a heart attack while crossing a bridge

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are sent to Bolivia, where they are challenged to travel through the rainforest and journey to the coast of Chile, driving second-hand off-road vehicles bought locally for a maximum of £3,500 each. Along the way, they encounter drug lords and feel the effects of altitude sickness as they negotiate the regions roads, one of which is a notoriously dangerous 40-mile mountain pass dubbed El Camino de la Muerte - the Death Road

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to America for an interstate road trip, completing various challenges along the way. Starting in Miami, they each buy a car for less than $1,000 and drive to New Orleans, where they set out to give their vehicles away to people who suffered in Hurricane Katrina

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a road trip, trying to follow the path of the Three Wise Men across the Middle East. However, they opt to do so in open-top sports cars that were built for significantly less demanding tasks than driving along the conflict-ridden mountains of southern Turkey, through war-ravaged Iraq and across the searing deserts of Syria. The trio also try to find out what myrrh is and where it can be bought

programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.desc: The escape of a dangerous prisoner ensures that DC Webbs first night duty turns out to be something of a baptism of fire. Chris Simmons stars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: George is threatened with legal action by a conman claiming to have suffered a severe allergic reaction to a homeopathic remedy. Kay admits to fraud when confronted by Daniel, and pressure at work takes its toll on Joe. Meanwhile, Nick is asked out on a date by a patient

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Mike helps a student who has a drink problem, while Mal lends a hand at the Campus. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, following the personal and professional lives of a group of GPs

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Tricia has a very emotional day comforting her friend Maggie Thornton in her final hours, and Mickie tries to console Mubbs, who is in a foul mood over Rosie and the baby. Will refuses to apologise to the canteen staff and has to endure taunts about his aristocratic background. As Jess is about to tell Sean and Ric the truth about her pregnancy, she is interrupted by Connie introducing the new head of anaesthetics - who happens to be a familiar face

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ruth has an interview to determine whether she will be fully reinstated as a doctor, but despite appearing confident, she buckles under the pressure when a stroke victim is brought into the ED. A hung-over Linda clashes with Tess over management styles, and a run-in with a grieving patient prompts her to reassess her attitude. Meanwhile, Jeff continues to mentor troubled teen Karl despite everyone considering him a lost cause. Starring Georgia Taylor

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The escape of a dangerous prisoner ensures that DC Webbs first night duty turns out to be something of a baptism of fire. Chris Simmons stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise is shocked to the core when Lucas reveals all. Elsewhere on the square, Leon and Zsa Zsa get closer, and a humiliated Peggy gets her own back on Glenda

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Libby battles her emotions, and the Square is aflame with gossip when the police make a revelation. Meanwhile, Glenda gets personal with Peggy, and Janine makes a bold decision

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan heads for trouble after castrating a pig and forgetting to dispose of the bits, and James provides some advice for a bridegroom. Guest starring Thora Hird and Warren Clarke, with Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: A prospector walks into the pub declaring he has struck gold while Blake and Danny play darts. However, shortly afterwards he is found dead in a mineshaft and Blake - who suffers from claustrophobia - is forced to go down to investigate. Meanwhile, Mattie is preparing to be a bridesmaid when her friends fiance Russ is arrested for murder, and he has an unlikely cellmate for the night. Detective drama, starring Craig McLachlan

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey gets a job as a secretary at a printing works, and her boss causes quite a stir. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg and his friends try to draw Gough away from his wife of 50 years for a bit of fun. Rural comedy, with Peter Sallis, Bill Owen, Michael Aldridge and Cardew Robinson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Getting Barry Higher in the World
programme.desc: A childs kite inspires Seymour to create his own giant version, and he tries to demonstrate to those around him how simple it is to make one. Michael Aldridge and Mike Grady star in Roy Clarkes long-running comedy

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora Batty invites Compo up to her bedroom, but it proves to be an entirely innocent invitation as she merely wishes him to remove a mangle and deliver it to a friend. However, the nature of the item creates all sorts of problems. Long-running comedy, starring Bill Owen and Kathy Staff

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: First episode of the Second World War comedy set in German-occupied France about the misadventures of womanising cafe owner Rene Artois, whose cafe is commandeered by the Resistance to shelter two airmen. Starring Gorden Kaye, Carmen Silvera and Vicki Michelle

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick is in the village trying to trace a valuable painting, so Rene and the Colonel decide to smuggle it out with the two British airmen who are disguised unconvincingly as Germans

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: A case of mistaken identity prompts communist partisans to capture the disguised airmen, leaving Rene to mastermind a rescue bid before all manner of mayhem breaks loose. Comedy written by Jeremy Lloyd and David Croft, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the case of criminal Mark Johnson, who was killed in a fire at Londons Union club in 1996. The original investigation was hampered by silence among witnesses and associates, but with new information suggesting the blaze was a targeted arson attack, the detectives hope they will have more success. Drama, guest starring Roy Marsden, Martin Marquez and Tom Watt

programme.title: Dalziel and Pascoe
programme.desc: An embalmed body is found washed up on Whitby beach, and turns out to be just the first in a spate of mysterious and seemingly unrelated deaths. The intrepid duos investigations turn up an equally bizarre set of leads including ex-Cold War spies, Draculas black dog and an old flame from Dalziels past. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When a local nun is strangled to death on her way to the chapel, the team find investigating the murder to be a real challenge after they learn that the members of her order have taken a vow of silence

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including the Royal Society for the Prevention of Mispronunciation and Stage Directions

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to apes and monkeys, revealing their similarities to humans. Large armies of hamadryas baboons battle on the plains of Ethiopia to steal females and settle old scores, while Japanese macaques beat the cold by lounging in thermal springs. An orang-utan baby fails in its struggle to make an umbrella out of leaves, and capuchins try to smash nuts with a large rock. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: An insight into mammals ability to learn new tricks to aid hunting prey and evading predators. Slow-motion cameras reveal the star-nosed moles technique for detecting its next meal underwater, while an ibex runs up a steep cliff face to escape a fox. A killer whale off the coast of the Falklands sneaks into a pool used by elephant seal pups and snatches them, saving itself the trouble of searching for food in the open sea, and three cheetah brothers work together to hunt a zebra. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to marine invertebrates - bizarre and beautiful animals that thrive in the toughest parts of the ocean. Divers swim into a shoal of predatory Humboldt squid emerging from the depths to hunt in packs, and timelapse photography reveals starfish gathering under the Arctic ice to devour a seal carcass. A camera also follows a female octopus into a cave, where she hides to protect her eggs until she starves to death

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The strategies adopted by plants that enable them to grow in every possible environment, even where there is no sunlight, water or nutrients. Featuring the dragons blood tree, which survives in the desert by converting moisture found in the mist and shading its own roots, the carnivorous Venus flytrap that has evolved into an extraordinary predator able to digest insects, and the cats claw creeper, which uses other plants as a ladder to reach sunlight. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to apes and monkeys, revealing their similarities to humans. Large armies of hamadryas baboons battle on the plains of Ethiopia to steal females and settle old scores, while Japanese macaques beat the cold by lounging in thermal springs. An orang-utan baby fails in its struggle to make an umbrella out of leaves, and capuchins try to smash nuts with a large rock. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.desc: Documentary drama charting the collapse of Charles Is rule in just 50 tempestuous days. Lisa Hilton relates how, after John Pym spurned Charles Is attempt to bring him onside with a job offer, the king was left with just one option - to arrest Pym and his colleagues, accusing them of high treason. But in his attempt to draw the politician out, the king played right into his enemys hands, appearing like a tyrant to all of Westminster

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britains finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Scientists battle to try to prove if the Turin Shroud is genuinely the burial cloth used to wrap Jesus dead body, or if its a fake

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Nazi Hunt for Holy Relics
programme.desc: How the Nazis searched for religious items. However, after their defeat, an American detachment known as ?The Monuments Men found and repatriated a large proportion of the loot

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Near Mount Ararat in Turkey is what scientists believe is a boat-shaped rock formation, but a research team hopes to prove its the remains of Noahs Ark

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstructions, historian Lisa Hilton discovers how in just 50 tempestuous days Charles Is rule collapsed, laying the foundations for civil war, the loss of royal power and ultimately, the Kings head

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: John Pym tries to pass parliamentary bills limiting the kings power, but in the Lords, the casting votes are held by the bishops, loyal to Charles. Pym knows that to succeed he must remove them, and they become his next target. In mid-December, his group goes for a political ambush, demanding that the Grand Remonstrance be published. They launch a surprise vote in the dead of night and win. Making matters worse for the King, rumours spread that the Queen was involved in the Irish rebellion

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Age of Steam
programme.desc: The steeplejack takes a look at the extraordinary expansion of industrial Britain in the 18th and 19th centuries, and the continued use of huge steam engines in collieries, steelworks and mills in the 20th century

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Age of Steam
programme.desc: Fred looks at early experiments in the use of steam for road transport, more than 100 years before the invention of the petrol engine. Plus, an insight into the development of the traction engine for agriculture and haulage

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: John Pym tries to pass parliamentary bills limiting the kings power, but in the Lords, the casting votes are held by the bishops, loyal to Charles. Pym knows that to succeed he must remove them, and they become his next target. In mid-December, his group goes for a political ambush, demanding that the Grand Remonstrance be published. They launch a surprise vote in the dead of night and win. Making matters worse for the King, rumours spread that the Queen was involved in the Irish rebellion

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: Documentary drama charting the collapse of Charles Is rule in just 50 tempestuous days. Lisa Hilton relates how, after John Pym spurned Charles Is attempt to bring him onside with a job offer, the king was left with just one option - to arrest Pym and his colleagues, accusing them of high treason. But in his attempt to draw the politician out, the king played right into his enemys hands, appearing like a tyrant to all of Westminster

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright loses his trousers on the way to the wedding of Nurse Gladys Emmanuels niece, and ends up having to borrow a pair of her trousers to wear at the event

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason returns as Granville in this follow-up to Roy Clarkes popular comedy Open All Hours, as he re-opens the doors of the nations favourite corner shop. In the opening episode, Arkwrights protege finds an old mangle in the yard - and wonders how he can best put it to use. Meanwhile, Beth continues to pressure Leroy to find a date for her best friend, Ruby, who has been suspended at work. Elsewhere, Eric and Cyril tutor Mr Newbold in the art of romance - and how to get closer to Mrs Rossi. Co-starring James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Katie Redford

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: A squeak on Granvilles bike interferes with his hopes of romance, and Arkwright worries about a mysterious visitor to a house in Atkinson Terrace

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville decides a fashion makeover is needed to impress Stephanie from the local boutique, but Arkwrights meddling soon puts paid to any romantic dalliance. Roy Clarkes classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma treats best friend Leanne to a shopping spree, during which she discovers Bolton Acting School and decides to join up. She also arranges another family meeting between Lin and Joan at an upmarket restaurant, but its not to everybodys taste. Comedy, starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: In a bid to make some much-needed money, Alison and Mike consider selling some land. The plan proves far from straight forward however, with Lady Button up in arms at the mere thought of losing some of her beloved estate. Thomas enters a competition that he hopes will finally see him become a published poet, but hearing Lady Button speak so passionately about Button House gives him an idea or two. Pat and Julian get locked in a dispute and it is Robin who offers a surprising pearl of wisdom. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the British sitcom, featuring interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show on its 20th anniversary

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin gets trapped between the joists in a loft while spending a weekend in a country cottage - and is eventually obliged to call Paul to his rescue. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan. From November 1987

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mavis goes to a wedding, but Gastric warns Granville of the danger of leaving her alone in a romantic setting. Eric, Cyril and Mr Newbold consider exploring their feminine side, while Mrs Featherstone is concerned that Mrs Rossi is becoming a bad influence on the shop, and Kath is on the receiving end of a new technique for matching customers to the perfect product

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: First episode of John Sullivans comedy series, following the lives of Only Fools and Horses characters Boycie and Marlene. Denzil tells Boycie the infamous Driscoll Brothers are after the supergrass who had them jailed - prompting the car salesman to sell his business and move to rural Shropshire. John Challis and Sue Holderness star, with a guest appearance by Paul Barber

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville starts making his own sausages using a second-hand machine. Gastric turns his Mini into a self-driving car, and Eric and Cyril take up rock climbing - but Kath insists they do their training at home. Mrs Featherstone decides to stir up some gossip in her pursuit of another husband, while Leroy tries to persuade Beth to break up with her boyfriend

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Bad-tempered Mr Grainger is deemed too long in the tooth to continue at Grace Brothers and calls are made for his resignation. Classic comedy, starring Arthur Brough and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Nurse Gladys decides Arkwright needs a lesson in politeness, and convinces him a writer for the Good Shop Guide is doing the rounds. She then poses as the critic to see for herself the unusual sight of the miserly shopkeeper being nice to his customers. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Lynda Baron

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio hatch a cunning plan to try to persuade a tight-fisted pub-goer to buy a round of drinks at the local

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Ballad for Wind Instruments and Canoe
programme.desc: The trio stumble across a canoe and decide to take it for an unauthorised trip up the river - but things soon start to go wrong. Meanwhile, Ivy wins a prize for having the best mouth-instrument

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends the christening of Daisys granddaughter, and is displeased to learn the childs parents are not married. With some encouragement from Richard, she agrees to keep an open mind, but is later irked when the mother and child go missing. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.sub-title: The Day the Balloon Went Up
programme.desc: The platoon rescues the verger from being carried away by a barrage balloon and the men take it off to the woods to secure it to a tree. Unfortunately they let go of the ropes too early and it floats off - taking Captain Mainwaring with it. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe, John Laurie, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: A mysterious woman comes looking for Eric, and Kath is not convinced by his claims to have no idea who she is. Granville buys a job lot of turnips, and tries to convince his customers of their remarkable medicinal qualities, and a taste of Mr Newbolds home-made wine leaves Mrs Featherstone feeling more determined than ever to find herself a fourth husband

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright loses his trousers on the way to the wedding of Nurse Gladys Emmanuels niece, and ends up having to borrow a pair of her trousers to wear at the event

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy is always looking for ways of improving her position in the world and sees another chance by befriending posh new neighbours Toby and Anna. But she hasnt reckoned on Lee dragging her right back down to earth. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star in the sitcom, with guest appearances from Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy becomes a relationship counsellor, so Lee and his father take advantage of her new-found skills in an effort to patch things up between them. Can they finally forgive and forget? Bobby Ball guest stars as Frank, with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Theres some good news for Lin as the hospital staff inform her that her section could finally be lifted, although this is dependent on her either moving into a hostel or in with a family member. As Alma is finally getting her life back on track and the hostel isnt exactly the right place for a recovering addict, Alma has to convince her grandma to let Lin move in with her - but will this be a step too far for Lin and Joan?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike are forced to cosy up to Barclay if their plan to sell some land to the golf consortium is going to work. Alison is involved in a silent negotiation of her own - she has designs on Lady Buttons bedroom but cant quite muster the courage to ask her outright if she would consider relocating. The ghosts become fixated on solving the mystery of Kittys death. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Martha Howe-Douglas

programme.desc: Mr Grace decides to move with the times by making an advertisement to be shown in local cinemas. Classic comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden. From April 1977

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring organises a dance in an attempt to boost platoon morale - but Pike interrupts proceedings to make an important announcement regarding his girlfriend Violet. Classic comedy by Jimmy Perry and David Croft, starring Ian Lavender. First broadcast in 1969

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright expands his empire by opening a mobile shop in a bid to impress Nurse Gladys Emmanuel, but the enterprise is cut short by a close encounter with some perishables

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville places an ad in a lonely hearts magazine and finally meets the girl of his dreams. After stretching the truth about being a young executive with a flash sports car, his romance ends before it even begins. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: In a desperate bid to prove his manliness, the happy-go-lucky Lee embarks on a camping trip with the object of his affections Lucy, his best mate Tim and his partner Daisy, but the spooky forest where they have chosen to pitch their tents could prove a little too intimidating for him to handle. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Tim Vine, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy decides she wants to have a baby, but what she didnt expect was for the whole family to take a personal interest in the conception. Lee thinks he should be the one, of course - so he cant believe it when she reveals she is planning to have the child with another man. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star, with Katy Wix, Bobby Ball, Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright expands his empire by opening a mobile shop in a bid to impress Nurse Gladys Emmanuel, but the enterprise is cut short by a close encounter with some perishables

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Nurse Gladys Emmanuels washing-machine breaks down, and Arkwright decides to try and lure her into his home with the offer of his vintage machine

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity shes been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The ghosts are officially bored, and Pat comes up with new entertainment for the gang. Mike and Alison host their international buyer at Button House in order to confirm the land sale, but Mikes antics threaten the whole deal - can he pull it back in time? Humphrey is offered a fresh perspective on his strained marriage and finally learns some French, with a little help from a most unexpected source

Granville buys a job lot of turnips, and tries to convince his customers of their remarkable medicinal qualities, and a taste of Mr Newbolds home-made wine leaves Mrs Featherstone feeling more determined than ever to find herself a fourth husband

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright loses his trousers on the way to the wedding of Nurse Gladys Emmanuels niece, and ends up having to borrow a pair of her trousers to wear at the event

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy is always looking for ways of improving her position in the world and sees another chance by befriending posh new neighbours Toby and Anna. But she hasnt reckoned on Lee dragging her right back down to earth. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star in the sitcom, with guest appearances from Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy becomes a relationship counsellor, so Lee and his father take advantage of her new-found skills in an effort to patch things up between them. Can they finally forgive and forget? Bobby Ball guest stars as Frank, with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out

Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Tim Vine, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy decides she wants to have a baby, but what she didnt expect was for the whole family to take a personal interest in the conception. Lee thinks he should be the one, of course - so he cant believe it when she reveals she is planning to have the child with another man. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star, with Katy Wix, Bobby Ball, Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma treats best friend Leanne to a shopping spree, during which she discovers Bolton Acting School and decides to join up. She also arranges another family meeting between Lin and Joan at an upmarket restaurant, but its not to everybodys taste. Comedy, starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: In a bid to make some much-needed money, Alison and Mike consider selling some land. The plan proves far from straight forward however, with Lady Button up in arms at the mere thought of losing some of her beloved estate. Thomas enters a competition that he hopes will finally see him become a published poet, but hearing Lady Button speak so passionately about Button House gives him an idea or two. Pat and Julian get locked in a dispute and it is Robin who offers a surprising pearl of wisdom. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Theres some good news for Lin as the hospital staff inform her that her section could finally be lifted, although this is dependent on her either moving into a hostel or in with a family member. As Alma is finally getting her life back on track and the hostel isnt exactly the right place for a recovering addict, Alma has to convince her grandma to let Lin move in with her - but will this be a step too far for Lin and Joan?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike are forced to cosy up to Barclay if their plan to sell some land to the golf consortium is going to work. Alison is involved in a silent negotiation of her own - she has designs on Lady Buttons bedroom but cant quite muster the courage to ask her outright if she would consider relocating. The ghosts become fixated on solving the mystery of Kittys death. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Martha Howe-Douglas

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee is looking forward to a lazy weekend when his dad turns up on the doorstep, looking for somewhere to stay having just come out of hospital - and in need of someone to care for him. Bobby Ball guest stars, with Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A wealthy new couple move into the block and soon find out that sharing a building with Lee and Lucy is anything but straightforward. The fun begins when Lee somehow manages to bag an invitation to a christening party being thrown by the couple - sending him and Lucy on a last-minute gift-buying trip, during which they make every wrong decision humanly possible to find the perfect present for the baby who seems to have everything. Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden join the cast, with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martins involvement in the local Neighbourhood Watch scheme irritates everyone - including the police. Comedy, starring Richard Briers

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity shes been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The ghosts are officially bored, and Pat comes up with new entertainment for the gang. Mike and Alison host their international buyer at Button House in order to confirm the land sale, but Mikes antics threaten the whole deal - can he pull it back in time? Humphrey is offered a fresh perspective on his strained marriage and finally learns some French, with a little help from a most unexpected source

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the British sitcom, featuring interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show on its 20th anniversary

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An active mum from a tight-knit pro wrestling family trades places with a mother-of-three whose husband prefers playing video games to spending time with his family

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A cosmopolitan, appearance-focused mum-of-three switches places with the mum from a down-to-earth farming family who has four children and hundreds of animals

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey challenges members of the Paine family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. There are emotional moments as single mother Steph and her three daughters go through their most treasured items, and make some difficult decisions about what to let go of from their past. The items they do decide to let go of will be put into piles to recycle, donate or sell. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from carpenter Robert Bent, organiser Dilly Carter and cleaner Iwan Carrington

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Pete meets a puppy who needs urgent treatment for a badly broken leg and a young Doberman who has spent most of her life in quarantine kennels

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery

Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family
programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, whos terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.sub-title: Hugo and the Ice Cream Pups
programme.desc: Pete meets some newborn pups fighting to survive, and helps teach Hugo, a Cane Corso rescued from quarantine at Dover, how to be a dog

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Flower, the first dog ever to take a dislike to him . He also helps Heidi and Marvin, both facing serious medical challenges

programme.title: The Supervet

Noel also treats an injured dog belonging to a friend from the gym

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of pioneering veterinary practice Fitzpatrick Referrals in Surrey, where orthopaedic-neuro veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick and his team help some of the nations hardest-to-cure pets using cutting-edge procedures. A woman arrives with 14 of her rescue dogs, including a tiny papillon in need of a double knee replacement. Noel and his team also fit a German shepherd with a specially designed bionic foot, and treat a mountain dog that is suffering problems with its spine

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: Police must defy all odds to find a missing four-year-old girl, and a teenager in Geelong runs away from home

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey encourages members of the Patel family to tidy their immediate environment, as mum Bijal, dad Darshan and their two children move all their possessions into a warehouse, where they are encouraged to recycle, donate and sell items. There are emotional moments as the quartet go through their most treasured items. Bijal and Darshan struggle to part with their possessions, but really want to create an environment free of the chaos for their son, who finds it hard to concentrate in the clutter. As usual, home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent offer tidying and upcycling tips

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Basset hound Barney is top dog at home, enjoying salmon and venison as some of his favourite treats. But lately Barneys vertebrae have been causing him problems and Noel Fitzpatrick needs to perform high-risk surgery using 3D-printed implants. The professor also treats a giant mastiff called Tank, who is suffering from severe arthritis and needs radical surgery on both elbows, and a family puppy who has fractured his foot jumping off a bed. Now he requires specially developed surgical treatment

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a border collie in a lot of pain with a suspicious lump on its ankle. However, the dogs recovery does not go to plan and proves extremely challenging. A short-haired cat arrives with a sore hip from jumping off the sofa, and it seems a hip replacement may be the best option, and a golden labrador is rushed into Fitzpatricks sister clinic in Guildford with a lump growing on its side

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: On Hull docks, the team are called in after a party of asylum seekers are discovered in a cargo container that arrived on an overnight ferry

programme.title: The Mormons Are Coming
programme.desc: Documentary following three young missionaries as they go through an intensive two-week induction at the Mormon training centre in Chorley, Lancashire. Giving up their first names to become known as Elders and Sisters, they have limited contact with family as they live under a strict timetable including role play and lessons on how to come across well to non-believers

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics treat a pregnant woman who has fallen on her baby bump. Plus, paramedics are called to an unconscious child with a complex medical condition

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: Detectives in Sydney launch a search for a tourist with life-threatening medical conditions, and the brother of a bestselling author goes missing in Victoria

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr transform the courtyard into a Mediterranean oasis. To brush up on their botany, the pair visit the magnificent gardens of one of Sicilys biggest wine producers, where they help out with the grape harvest. Keen to create an authentic Sicilian design, Alan explores Sciaccas famous ceramic scene and gets arty painting Moor Heads. He also enlists the help of two baroque-style painters to bring a touch of the islands rich history with a surprise courtyard centrepiece. Amanda repairs the old courtyard walls, which turns out to be a far bigger job than anticipated

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Flower, the first dog ever to take a dislike to him . He also helps Heidi and Marvin, both facing serious medical challenges

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of pioneering veterinary practice Fitzpatrick Referrals in Surrey, where orthopaedic-neuro veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick and his team help some of the nations hardest-to-cure pets using cutting-edge procedures. A woman arrives with 14 of her rescue dogs, including a tiny papillon in need of a double knee replacement. Noel and his team also fit a German shepherd with a specially designed bionic foot, and treat a mountain dog that is suffering problems with its spine

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An active mum from a tight-knit pro wrestling family trades places with a mother-of-three whose husband prefers playing video games to spending time with his family

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A cosmopolitan, appearance-focused mum-of-three switches places with the mum from a down-to-earth farming family who has four children and hundreds of animals

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: Detectives in Sydney launch a search for a tourist with life-threatening medical conditions, and the brother of a bestselling author goes missing in Victoria

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Basset hound Barney is top dog at home, enjoying salmon and venison as some of his favourite treats. But lately Barneys vertebrae have been causing him problems and Noel Fitzpatrick needs to perform high-risk surgery using 3D-printed implants. The professor also treats a giant mastiff called Tank, who is suffering from severe arthritis and needs radical surgery on both elbows, and a family puppy who has fractured his foot jumping off a bed. Now he requires specially developed surgical treatment

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a border collie in a lot of pain with a suspicious lump on its ankle. However, the dogs recovery does not go to plan and proves extremely challenging. A short-haired cat arrives with a sore hip from jumping off the sofa, and it seems a hip replacement may be the best option, and a golden labrador is rushed into Fitzpatricks sister clinic in Guildford with a lump growing on its side

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: On Hull docks, the team are called in after a party of asylum seekers are discovered in a cargo container that arrived on an overnight ferry

programme.title: The Mormons Are Coming
programme.desc: Documentary following three young missionaries as they go through an intensive two-week induction at the Mormon training centre in Chorley, Lancashire. Giving up their first names to become known as Elders and Sisters, they have limited contact with family as they live under a strict timetable including role play and lessons on how to come across well to non-believers

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: Police must defy all odds to find a missing four-year-old girl, and a teenager in Geelong runs away from home

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and broadcaster Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of Americas most notorious female killers in a quest to discover what drives women to kill. He travels to Gatesville, Texas, to visit Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her entire family at the age of 16, and will remain behind bars until she is almost 60. Piers finds out what made a typical teenager conspire with her boyfriend to kill her mum, dad, and two younger brothers, before meeting the only survivor of the tragedy, Erins father Terry

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr transform the courtyard into a Mediterranean oasis. To brush up on their botany, the pair visit the magnificent gardens of one of Sicilys biggest wine producers, where they help out with the grape harvest. Keen to create an authentic Sicilian design, Alan explores Sciaccas famous ceramic scene and gets arty painting Moor Heads. He also enlists the help of two baroque-style painters to bring a touch of the islands rich history with a surprise courtyard centrepiece. Amanda repairs the old courtyard walls, which turns out to be a far bigger job than anticipated

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earths great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planets breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earths surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Back from honeymoon, Bob and Thelma become increasingly worried about Terry, who seems to have gone off beer and mellowed into a strange state of middle-aged contemplativeness. Rodney Bewes and James Bolam star

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.sub-title: Mini Cooper and Chevy Pick Up
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business. Featured vehicles include a Mini Cooper and Chevrolet Pick-up truck

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims, beginning in the city of Orleans in north-central France. Here, he follows his Bradshaws guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, which tell the story of the heroine of France, Joan of Arc. Later, Michael takes a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car, before making his way to the capital city of Paris. East of Paris, Michael finishes this leg of his journey in style with a tour of the cellars at Domaine Pommery in Reims

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaws Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlins Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schoneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movements first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturers Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team pay over the odds for a VW Campervan, but can they hope to break even when they turn out a lovely bus for auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Enola Gay drops the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima in August 1945 as America bids to secure an unconditional surrender from the Japanese, even though conventional bombing raids had already flattened the country. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them in the Streets
programme.desc: The physical defence of London was crucial, and a ring of steel and concrete was thrown up around the capital as well as utilising natural defences

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Chester Cathedral, where they meet 2,000 eager visitors in the nave. Among the treasures coming under scrutiny are some of the smallest pieces of furniture ever seen on the show, an Elvis hound dog which almost got the King in hot water with the police, and jewels smuggled out of pre-revolution Russia sewn into the hem of a dress

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In the Pavilion at Ascot Racecourse, a small coin dug up by a young visitor yields a surprise valuation, and a model of a champion racehorse brings the house down. The first document-copying machine, dating back more than 200 years, is also brought in for inspection by the experts. Fiona Bruce presents

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Three rare Alvises get the auction buyers going when put under the hammer. A Chevy Corvette which started its journey in Saudi Arabia makes its way to Thornton le Dale for the sale

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave gets a nasty surprise when he opens the bonnet of a fast Ford from the 80s, and an Austin Heavy 12 nearly a century old sets off a bidding war at the auction

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Enola Gay drops the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima in August 1945 as America bids to secure an unconditional surrender from the Japanese, even though conventional bombing raids had already flattened the country. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them in the Streets
programme.desc: The physical defence of London was crucial, and a ring of steel and concrete was thrown up around the capital as well as utilising natural defences

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Along the way he discovers the history of the Ashes at Melbourne cricket ground, learns about the exploits of outlaw Ned Kelly and visits the home of superstar soprano Dame Nellie Melba. He also rides on the Puffing Billy narrow gauge steam railway, built in 1900, and has a go at shearing a sheep

programme.title: Locomotion: Dan Snows History of Railways
programme.desc: The historian and broadcaster examines how in just 50 years, Britains railways grew from a handful of small lines carrying coal to become the nations biggest industry. The emergence of the rail network changed working conditions for British employees, proved a valuable export across the globe and even changed the way war was waged

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: David Attenborough journeys into the hidden world of forests, from the temperate rainforests of Canada, where rarely seen spirit bears fish for salmon, to the teak forests of India, where whistling wild dogs work together to bring down prey three times their size. In the misty mountainous forests of China, male tragopan have developed a comical dance routine, whilst in the dense tropical rainforest, treehoppers form surprising alliances to fight off assassin bugs and oriental pied hornbills go to incredible lengths to protect their young

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want to buy an MGB roadster but the cheaper GT version is the one they find. Its got a few rust patches, and back in the workshop they realise beauty is skin deep

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Skippers Ian Ritchie and John Stevenson endure ferocious weather and problems with a new net, while Charles and Charlie McBride worry when they catch too much cod in the North Sea after receiving penalties in the past for over-fishing. Over on the Viking Monarch, hopes are high for a bright future

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: John Stevenson and Ian Ritchie are forced to take shelter in Bergen, Norway, before an engine failure and a dramatic mercy dash make their trips even worse. Ocean Venture skipper John Buchan Sr seeks exotic species in the Atlantic and has a close encounter with a Spanish trawler

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a railway journey from the capital Palermo, through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracusa, to Europes largest volcano, Mount Etna, Michael Portillo explores Sicilian life under the dictatorship. He also finds out how the dictator took on the Mafia and asks whether it is true that under Mussolini, the trains ran on time

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers evidence of the German occupation of the Jersey village of Gorey and the role it played in defending the UK. He meets local historian Phillip Marett, who takes him to the gun tower from where the occupying soldiers could have watched the Battle of Normandy, and hears a first-hand recollection of the liberation of the island

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers how the Cornish village of Botallack was the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry and supplied a deadly poison to the world. Local resident Paula Nicholls explains how her ancestors, the Boyne family, owned a mine which was involved in a catastrophic explosion in 1893 where 20 men tragically died

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team set out on a pre-war challenge with a 1939 three-wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team invest in a piece of French art this week - a 1971 Citroen DS21 Pallas

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britains military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them in the Fields
programme.desc: Numerous pill boxes, gun emplacements and dragons teeth were all tactically positioned to slow the Germans trying to get to the major cities

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A selection of finds from visits to Althorp and Ascot. Items include rare documents related to the rescue of the Russian royal family by a British battleship during the revolution, modern paintings found in a skip, and an early English tapestry of unexpected value. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Lanhydrock near Bodmin in Cornwall, where they unearth treasures including a gold bangle inlaid with precious stones discovered at the bottom of a water tank, a silver cup, and an early Valentine that tells a romantic tale

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek, with help from the Mathewson clan, drags a mini pickup out of the barn where its been for 20 years, and a 350cc Douglas Mark V motorbike from 1950 goes under the hammer

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britains military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the worlds most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Veteran naturalist David Attenborough explores the fragile marine ecosystem of the coral reef, where all creatures, including the corals themselves, have to fight for survival. From tiny beginnings, the reef grows and develops over hundreds of years, providing support for innumerable animals - yet one storm can threaten the entire community

programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them in the Fields
programme.desc: Numerous pill boxes, gun emplacements and dragons teeth were all tactically positioned to slow the Germans trying to get to the major cities

programme.desc: Derek goes to pick up a veteran 1904 Swift, theres an emotional farewell to a 1980s Bedford HA van, and a Toyota Corona finds a new home

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has to extract a vintage car from its bizarre resting place, while Charlie picks up a Vauxhall Viva from a man who has owned it for over half a century

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways in which animals battle to survive in a world of extremes, from mountain summits to deserts, polar tundra to the worlds largest cave in Vietnam. On Ellesmere Island, a pack of Arctic wolves fight to rebuild their strength after one of the toughest winters on record, while in the mountains of Mexico, millions of Monarch butterflies huddle together to survive. But the calm is shattered when a storm hits their forest shelter

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of fresh water around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the worlds driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Deserts bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus Gregory and Will Trickett want to buy a BMW, and Will says he likes the feminine Z3. Theres a trade-in up the road which might fit the bill but its a high price. Can they get the deal they want?
